info.name: BookMyShow API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the BookMyShowOct24 Spring Boot app.

## Two ways to test

### 1. Individual endpoints (Users / Movies / Shows / Bookings / Payments / Coupons folders)
One-off testing of a single endpoint. Edit the body or the collection variable and fire it.

### 2. Simulated Agent Flow folder ⭐ (recommended for end-to-end testing)
The same REST endpoints the AI agent's tools wrap, called in the order the agent would call them. This means:
- **No Anthropic API key needed.** You're not hitting `/api/agent/chat`; you're calling the services directly.
- **Deterministic.** The LLM is non-deterministic; this flow isn't.
- **IDs chain automatically** — each request has a test script that captures the id it needs for the next request.
- **Each step names the agent tool it simulates** so you can see the mapping (`SearchMoviesTool` → `GET /api/movies`, etc.).

Run the **Simulated Agent Flow** folder top-down with the Collection Runner (or one request at a time). The chain is:

```
Sign Up  →  search_movies  →  get_shows  →  get_show_seats
         →  create_booking  →  apply_coupon  →  process_payment
         →  (optional) cancel_booking
```

## Before you run

1. Start the Spring Boot app. Default port is `8080` (matches the `baseUrl` variable).
2. Seed at least one movie + show + seats into the DB so the search returns something (otherwise the later steps will have nothing to chain against).
3. If you do want to test the live LLM agent too, set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` before starting the app, and uncomment the Live Agent folder below (currently empty).

## Collection variables

- `baseUrl` — `http://localhost:8080`
- `userId` — authenticated user id (auto-captured after Sign Up)
- `movieId`, `showId`, `showSeatId1`, `showSeatId2`, `bookingId`, `finalAmount` — auto-captured from the chained responses
- `searchName`, `searchCity` — override to change what the simulated search looks for

item.name: Simulated Agent Flow (no LLM needed)
item.description:
End-to-end booking flow that simulates what the AI agent would orchestrate — but bypasses the LLM. Each request below corresponds to exactly one AgentTool, calling the same service the tool wraps.

**Mapping:**

| Step | Agent Tool | REST endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | (prerequisite) | `POST /api/users/signup` |
| 1 | `SearchMoviesTool` | `GET /api/movies` |
| 2 | `GetShowsTool` | `GET /api/movies/{id}/shows` |
| 3 | `GetShowSeatsTool` | `GET /api/shows/{id}/seats` |
| 4 | `CreateBookingTool` | `POST /api/bookings` |
| 5 | `ApplyCouponTool` | `POST /api/coupons/apply` |
| 6 | `ProcessPaymentTool` | `POST /api/payments` |
| 7 | `CancelBookingTool` | `DELETE /api/bookings/{id}` |

**How to run**: open Postman's Collection Runner, select just this folder, click **Run**. Or run each request manually top-down — the variables chain via test scripts so later requests pick up the ids captured by earlier ones.

exec: // Sign up may succeed OR may fail with 'email already exists' on re-runs.
// Both are fine for this flow — we just need the userId variable set.
// If your service doesn't return userId, hardcode `userId` in the collection variables.

request.description: Create a user. Run once; subsequent runs will fail with 'email already exists' (harmless for this flow — the `userId` variable just needs to be set). If the signup response doesn't include `userId`, hardcode it via the collection variable panel.

exec: // This simulates the agent calling SearchMoviesTool.
// Capture the first movie's id so get_shows can use it.

request.description:
LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"search_movies","input":{"name":"push","city":"Mumbai"}}
```
Here we send the same parameters as query args. Test script saves `movies[0].id` into `movieId` for the next step.

exec: // Capture the first show's id so get_show_seats can use it.

request.description:
LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"get_shows","input":{"movieId":1,"city":"Mumbai"}}
```
Uses `movieId` captured from step 1. Saves `shows[0].id` into `showId`.

exec: // Grab the first two AVAILABLE seats for the booking step.

request.description:
LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"get_show_seats","input":{"showId":1}}
```
Response contains seats with `showSeatStatus` = AVAILABLE / BLOCKED / BOOKED. Test script picks the first two AVAILABLE ones for the booking.

exec: // Capture booking.id so payment + cancel steps can use it.

request.description:
LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"create_booking","input":{"showSeatIds":[501,502]}}
```
(Note: the LLM version gets `userId` from `AgentContext`, not from the tool input. Here we send it in the body since the plain REST endpoint takes it.)

Moves seats from AVAILABLE → BLOCKED. State machine (if agent were involved): IDLE → BOOKING_PENDING.

exec: // Capture finalAmount so process_payment charges the discounted total.

request.description:
LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"apply_coupon","input":{"code":"FIRST50","amount":1000}}
```
Returns `{code, originalAmount, discount, finalAmount}`. Test script stores `finalAmount` so the next step charges the discounted total.

LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"process_payment","input":{"bookingId":42,"paymentMode":"UPI","amount":1000}}
```
Moves seats from BLOCKED → BOOKED and the booking from PENDING → CONFIRMED. State machine (if agent were involved): BOOKING_PENDING → BOOKING_CONFIRMED.

Payment modes: UPI, CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, NET_BANKING.

LLM would emit:
```json
{"tool":"cancel_booking","input":{"bookingId":42}}
```
Frees the seats and marks booking as cancelled. Fails if the show starts in less than 60 minutes (BookingService guard).

State machine (if agent were involved): any → IDLE.
Body on GET is unusual but that's how the controller is written.

item.name: Movies
item.description: Standalone movie search + shows for a movie.

query.description: ENGLISH | HINDI | TAMIL | TELUGU | KANNADA | MALAYALAM | MARATHI | BENGALI | PUNJABI | SPANISH | FRENCH

request.description: Valid payment modes: UPI, CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, NET_BANKING.

variable.description: Authenticated user id. Auto-populated by step 0 of the Simulated Agent Flow if signup returns a userId; otherwise set manually.
Movie name to search for in step 1 of the flow.
City to filter by in steps 1 and 2 of the flow.
Auto-captured from step 3 (get_show_seats). First AVAILABLE seat.
Auto-captured from step 3 (get_show_seats). Second AVAILABLE seat.
Auto-captured from step 5 (apply_coupon). Used as the payment amount in step 6.
